heading: Access to Federal insurance
label: 1647b Access to Federal insurance
text: Notwithstanding the provisions of title 5, Executive order, or administrative regulation, an Indian tribe or tribal organization carrying out programs under the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act ( et seq.) or the Tribally Controlled Schools Act of 1988 ( et seq.) or an urban Indian organization carrying out programs under subchapter IV of this chapter shall be entitled to purchase coverage, rights, and benefits for the employees of such Indian tribe or tribal organization, or urban Indian organization, under chapter 89 of title 5 and chapter 87 of such title if necessary employee deductions and agency contributions in payment for the coverage, rights, and benefits for the period of employment with such Indian tribe or tribal organization, or urban Indian organization, are currently deposited in the applicable Employee’s Fund under such title.25 U.S.C. 530125 U.S.C. 2501
notes: References in TextThe Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act, referred to in text, is , , , which is classified principally to chapter 46 (§ 5301 et seq.) of this title. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see , set out as a Short Title note under and Tables.Pub. L. 93–63888 Stat. 2203section 1 of Pub. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.Pub. L. 100–297102 Stat. 385section 2501 of this titleApr. 28, 1988
